data.title: Weak labor market data may prompt Fed rate cuts, pushing stocks to new highs. Job adds at 73k in July, and 75k expected in August. Wall Street eyes record highs.
data.description: The stock market, particularly the S&P 500, is poised to benefit from the recent surge in bad economic news. The deteriorating labor market conditions have increased the likelihood of upcoming interest rate cuts, which could propel stocks to new record highs. Following a slight dip from its previous peak, the S&P 500, represented by the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (NYSE: VOO), has recovered vigorously and is now merely 0.5% below its all-time high. Investors are optimistic about the prospect of weak job data prompting the Federal Reserve to ease its monetary policy potentially this month.July's labor market report revealed a significant disappointment, adding only 73,000 jobs, well below the necessary monthly range for solid employment growth. Economists anticipate another poor showing in the upcoming non-farm payrolls report for August, with projections suggesting just 75,000 new jobs. The general sentiment in the market is leaning towards a positive response to such negative labor market indicators, as investors view them as potential triggers for Federal Reserve intervention.
Fed's Williams: The Fed needs to keep the economy on track and allow tariffs to pass through.
